ks1.objective: Underarm throw to your partner and catch with two hands.
successCriteria: Throws are soft and aimed at the partner
Both partners take turns to throw

lks2.objective: Build smooth, controlled throwing and two-handed catching — step back as accuracy improves.
successCriteria: Pair counts 10 cleans before stepping back

uks2.objective: Refine the kinetic chain on throws and adjust body position for low or high catches.
successCriteria: Throws step forward with opposite foot
Adjusts hand shape: thumbs together (high), little fingers together (low)

space.description: Pairs spread out, 3–4m apart. Clear ground — no slopes or rough patches.

steps: Underarm throw, catch with two hands.
After 10 clean catches, take a step back.
questions: What helps you catch the ball cleanly every time?
Where should your hands be before the ball arrives?

errors.issue: Closes eyes at point of catch
errors.cause: Fear of the ball; tracking breaks
errors.fix: Softer ball, closer distance, "say catch as it lands"
Cue "thumbs together" for high catches, "little fingers together" for low

step.space: Closer for less able, further for more able
step.task: Add an overarm throw after 5 clean catches
step.equipment: Larger / softer ball for Y1–2; tennis ball for Y5–6
step.people: Move into threes for triangle passing

questions: What changes when you have to throw further?
How does your body position change when fielding low vs high?
